crop_context: multiple crops across global field studies
observed_response: Positive physiological and yield-linked responses are common, but field performance varies with management and environment
recommendation_signal: medium-to-high confidence only with context labeling
notes: Useful as the broad anchor for comparison but not enough for crop-specific recommendation.

field_context: field translation from lab to land
observed_response: Translation failures are strongly linked to pH, root exudate specificity, survival, and environmental variability
recommendation_signal: avoid blind deployment and label low-confidence contexts
notes: Important negative-control logic for the framework.

field_context: in-field inoculation with pre-existing soil microbiome
observed_response: Soil microbiome indicators can predict where AMF response is more likely to be positive
notes: Key source for turning intervention choice into response-aware logic.

intervention_components: biological inputs plus traditional and reduced-mineral approaches
observed_response: Integrated approaches are framed as more robust than single-input dependency, especially under sustainability constraints
recommendation_signal: medium-to-high confidence for mixed intervention framing
notes: Useful for framing intervention classes beyond inoculants alone.

observed_response: Dual inoculation improved soil fertility indicators and nutrient availability in field-grown forage legumes
recommendation_signal: high confidence for combination logic in similar semi-arid legume contexts
notes: Good support for combination-over-single intervention reasoning.

observed_response: Crop diversification and fertilization strategies generally enhanced microbial abundance and functional gene patterns, with site variability
recommendation_signal: medium confidence for systems-level intervention logic with strong site dependence
notes: Useful for showing that not all intervention comparison belongs to product-level bioinputs.
